observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue a directive to MDRRMC to incorporate all expenditures on all programs, projects, and activities in the LDRRMFIP as required in Section 5.1.2 COA Circular No. 2012-002, and the MDRMMO to monitor the completeness of the LDRRMFIP submitted.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM Council, with the efforts of the MDRRMO, was able to secure and incorporate all necessary expenditures in the LDRRMFIP. Moreover, the office sorted the unspent portion by year and reverted to the General Fund all those that had already lapsed after the 5-year period;

observations.recommendation:
Require the Human Resource Management Office to provide adequate manpower and resources to conduct a complete physical count of PPE.
Require the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor on or before January 31 of the following year.
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Review and reclassify PPE items below ₱50,000 as semi-expendable property in compliance with COA Circular No. 2022-004. Adjustments should be made to ensure accurate financial reporting; and
Keep PPELC updated and complete.
Require GSO to keep PPEPC likewise updated and complete, and to conduct regular reconciliation with the Accounting Office for accurate property records.
Implement the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts procedures as provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish verifiable PPE balances as to existence, condition, and accountability.

3.12. We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require coordination between the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer for the detailed breakdown and analysis of the Due to NGAs account;
Require the Municipal Accountant to confer with the source agency and prepare confirmation letters to determine if the LGU has pending unliquidated balances; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to verify transactions that were recorded in the cashbook and prepare schedules/reports if the remaining balances pertain to unexpended/unutilized balance of fund transfer and reconcile with the accounting records. Likewise, issue check for the refund to the source agency.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following measures:
Require the Municipal General Services Officer to properly record, monitor, and maintain inventory records for procured items, ensuring that issuances are supported by appropriate documents such as Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued.
b. 	Require the Municipal Accountant to perform the following:
Stop the practice of recording the procured inventory items/goods as outright expense to prevent misstatement of both the inventory and expense accounts; and at the same time, strengthen the inventory control and accountability mechanism; and
Effect the correct accounting entry in setting up inventory for procured items and supplies as prescribed in the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and the Local DRRM Officer to:
Refrain from using DRRMF for programs/projects/activities (PPAs) which are not directly related with pre-disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations to ensure that it is utilized solely for approved DRRM-related PPAs;
Maximize DRRM projects and allocate sufficient resources towards disaster prevention, mitigation, preparedness, and response to significantly decrease the community’s vulnerability to disaster risks; and
Utilize the Fund strictly for disaster risk management activities in accordance with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. 	Direct the Local Development Council to monitor and expedite the implementation of the remaining projects under the 20% LDF to adequately deliver the basic services to the constituents and maximize its benefits to the intended beneficiaries in compliance with the provisions of Section 5 of DILG-DBM Circular No. 2017-01 and RA No. 7160 through the following remedial measures; and
b. 	Require coordination between the End-user, the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), and other concerned offices to ensure that the procurement activities of the PPAs charged to the 20% Development Fund are well planned as required in DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) through the BAC Secretariat to comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 and pertinent provisions of the IRR of RA 9184. Also, submit the required supporting documents by complying with the contract review checklist to facilitate the review thereof.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. 	Submit the Report of Check Issued (RCI), Report of Disbursement (RD), and Liquidation Report (LR) together with the required supporting documents on the disbursements of funds; and the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) together with the Duplicate Copies of the Accountable Forms issued and deposit slips weekly or within 5 days at the end of the month to the Municipal Accounting Office.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. 	Submit the lacking disbursement vouchers complete with relevant supporting documents; and
b. 	Keenly observe the deadline set for the submission of trial balances and other financial reports as required under Sections 122 and 127 of PD 1445, Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-006, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006.

10.6. 	We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a.  The GAD Focal Person to actively coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the responsibility center (RC) and RC Code for GFPS are properly established in the financial systems in compliance with COA Circular No. 2021-008;
b. The Municipal Accountant, in collaboration with the Municipal Budget Officer, establishes a distinct RC and RC code for the GFPS, ensuring that these are reflected in the computerized accounting system and budget systems; and
c. The Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the budget for GAD-related programs, activities, and expenses is properly aligned with the RC for GFPS.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit copies of the Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly to facilitate the timely verification of the Cash-in-Bank accounts; and

Hire additional staff in the Accounting Office to address the delay in the submission of the monthly required Financial Reports.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant was able to prepare and submit copies of the Bank Reconciliation Statements to facilitate the timely verification of the Cash-in-Bank accounts. Cash Accounts were reconciled monthly by the check books /records of the Municipal Treasurer and the reports of the Municipal Accountant.

Additional staff in the Accounting Office were augmented or hired.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management the following measures:

a. 	Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs and submit the complete RPCPPE for all types of property of the LGU;

b. 	Require the GSO to maintain the required Property Cards, respectively for all types of PPE owned by the LGU, and update and reconcile their property records with that of the Accounting Office’s Property Ledger Cards; and

c. 	To avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.managementAction:
Established Inventory Committee and Appraisal Committee. Physical count on various PPEs is still ongoing.


The GSO maintains the required Property Cards for all types of PPE owned by the LGU, and duly updates and reconciles their property records with the Accounting Office’s Property Ledger Cards.
observations.reason:
Physical count on various PPEs is still ongoing.

Ongoing preliminary activities for one-time cleansing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Strictly comply with the rules and regulations under COA Circular No. 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023, prescribing the Updated Documentary Requirement for Common Government Transactions.

Require the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize the completeness of transactions, disbursement vouchers be supported with complete documentation before payment shall be made.

Direct the OIC-Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Accounting Office and submit the required supporting documents that emanate from his office.

Instruct the BAC and BAC-Secretariat to strictly comply with the requirements under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, on the submission of contracts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

a. 	Ensure that the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality be utilized only to fund priority programs or projects included in the approved Annual Investment Plan (AIP) which partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in compliance and in accordance with the provisions DBM-DOF- DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to ensure its optimal utilization;

b. 	Require the MPDC to regularly monitor the 20% development projects to ensure the efficient and effective implementation thereof; and submit accomplishment report to the Audit Team.

c. 	Direct the BAC-Secretariat to submit the documents relative to the conduct of contract review and the Municipal Engineer to prepare a letter request for technical inspection of the said projects; and

d. 	Augment additional staff in the BAC-Secretariat to assist in the compilation, segregation and preparation of all supporting documents due for submission to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction:
The Project Monitoring Team under the Office of the Municipal Engineer was re-organized, in which the Municipal Engineer was given the discretion to delegate, assign, monitor and implement the 20% Development Fund Projects to ensure the efficient and effective implementation as planned.




The Bids and Awards Committee was able to submit Contract Reviews for CY2023 and 2024 Projects.




Additional staff in the BAC-Secretariat were augmented to assist in the compilation, segregation and preparation of all supporting documents due for submission to the Audit Team.

The HRMO Officer posted the vacant position of Municipal Assessor. Still, there is no qualified applicant as of this date.






The basis of RPT used by the Municipal Accountant in establishing and reconciling the RPT balances is the reports submitted by the Municipal Treasurer. This is to recognize the appropriate amount of the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year. 


The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconciled their records and made the appropriate adjustments to reflect the correct balances.





Despite the absence of a Municipal Assessor, the Municipal Treasurer religiously   submits the necessary report to the Accounting Office to enable the Municipal Accountant to recognize the amount of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts at the
 beginning of the year.
observations.reason:
The HRMO Officer posted the vacancy in accordance with CSC rules however, there is no qualified applicant as per verification.




Updated Real Property Tax Account Register and Real Property Tax Index Cards are still not submitted for verification.

observations.managementAction: The Management had partially implemented the measures. The GAD focal personnel gathered some sex-disaggregated data and initiated consolidation. However, due to complexity and lack of proficiency of GAD personnel and lack of proper procedures, the database was not fully institutionalized. Accomplishment Report was not submitted to the Auditor within the period prescribed under Item V of COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
observations.reason: Due to complexity of GAD and lack of competence, lack of proficiency of GAD personnel and lack of proper procedures.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management the following measures:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs and submit the complete RPCPPE for all types of property of the LGU;
b. Require the GSO to maintain the required Property Cards, respectively for all types of PPE owned by the LGU, and update and reconcile their property records with that of the Accounting Office’s Property Ledger Cards; and
c. To avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. Strictly comply with the rules and regulations under COA Circular No. 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023, prescribing the Updated Documentary Requirement for Common Government Transactions.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize the completeness of transactions, disbursement vouchers be supported with complete documentation before payment shall be made.
c. Direct the OIC-Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Accounting Office and submit the required supporting documents that emanate from his office.
d. Instruct the BAC and BAC-Secretariat to strictly comply the requirements under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 on the submission of contracts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issues a directive to MDRRMC to incorporate all expenditures on all programs, projects, and activities in the LDRRMFIP as required in Section 5.1.2 COA Circular No. 2012-002 and the MDRMMO to monitor the completeness of the LDRRMFIP submitted.

We recommended that Management require the HRMO Officer to post and fill in the vacant position as prescribed in Sections 443 and 472 of RA 7160 and in accordance with existing guidelines, rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission, and adopt innovative ways to ensure there is a wider publication and posting.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
• Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit copies of the Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly to facilitate the timely verification of the Cash-in-Bank accounts; and
• Hire additional staff in the Accounting Office to address the delay of the submission of the monthly required Financial Reports.

observations.managementAction:
Quitclaims were duly signed by the HCW.
Claims were processed with the Accomplishment Report duly signed and verified by the immediate supervisors before processing claims for payment in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001;

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. Require the General Services Officer to submit the complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) on or before January 31 of each year, copy furnished the COA Auditor concerned; and to prepare and maintain Property Cards for each category of PPE accounts to substantiate the existence, completeness, and reliability of items composing the respective PPE accounts;
b. Require the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their inventory records and review the composition of records to come up with an accurate classification of Property, Plant, and Equipment account balance; and
c. Direct the Inventory Committee, the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to observe COA Circular No. 2020-006 prescribing the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of property, plant and equipment, recognition of PPE items found at station, and the disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of Government Agencies for proper planning and its corresponding implementation to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.managementAction:
Partial submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). The GSO and Appraisal Inventory Committee Established.
Initially conducted Inventory Count. Assets were still ongoing. The GSO maintains the required Property Cards for all types of PPE owned by the LGU, and duly updates and reconciles their property records with the Accounting Office’s Property Ledger Cards.
The one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 was not yet implemented.
observations.reason: Need more time in conducting inventory count, considering the office relocation.

observations.managementAction:
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) of Nunungan, Lanao del Norte was re-organized, in which the BAC Chairman was assigned to the Municipal Engineer.
The Bids and Awards Committee thru the BAC Secretariat was able to submit Contract Reviews for Projects funded by DBM based on the documentary requirement for common government transactions.
The Municipal Accountant continuously scrutinize the completeness of transactions; disbursement vouchers were supported with complete documentation before payment shall be made. Any unexpended amount was reverted back to the National Agency (DBM) thru Bureau of Treasury.
The Municipal Engineer coordinated with the Accounting Office and submitted required supporting documents that emanate from his office.

The MPDC monitors the 20% Development projects to ensure the efficient and effective implementation as planned.
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) of Nunungan, Lanao del Norte was re-organized, in which the BAC Chairman was assigned to the Municipal Engineer. The BAC Secretariat was able to submit the documents relative to the conduct of contract review for CY2023 and CY2024 projects. However, the Municipal Engineer was unable to prepare letter request for technical inspection of the said projects at that time.
Additional staff in the BAC-Secretariat was augmented to assist in the compilation, segregation and preparation of all supporting documents due for submission to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer was unable to prepare letter request for technical inspection of the said projects. Submission delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue a directive to MDRRMC to incorporate all expenditures on all programs, projects, and activities in the LDRRMFIP as required in Section 5.1.2 COA Circular No. 2012-002 and the MDRMMO to monitor the completeness of the LDRRMFIP submitted.
observations.managementAction:
Sorted the unspent portion by year and reverted to the General Fund all those that have already lapsed after the 5-year period. The MDRRMO with the efforts of the MDRRM Council with the Budget Office was able to secure and incorporate all necessary expenditures in the LDRRMFIP. The Accountant was able to classify and sort the unspent portion by year as well as the reversal to the General Fund.
The MDRRMO was able to monitor the completeness of the LDRRMFIP submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the HRMO Officer to post and fill in the vacant position as prescribed in Sections 443 and 472 of RA 7160 and in accordance with existing guidelines, rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission, and adopt innovative ways to ensure there is a wider publication and posting.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO Officer posted the vacant position of the Municipal Assessor but there is still no qualified applicant as of date. The HRMO continues efforts in posting the vacancy.
observations.reason: There is no qualified applicant for the position as of date despite the efforts of the HRMO in posting the vacant position.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMC to incorporate all expenditures on all programs, projects, and activities in the LDRRMFIP as required in Section 5.1.2 COA Circular No. 2012-002; require the Accounting Office to properly classify or sort the unspent portion by year and revert to the General Fund those that have lapsed after the 5-year period; direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain a separate registry for the appropriations, allotments, and obligations of the LDRRMF; and create the mandatory support staff as mandated under Section 12.b of RA 10121 to assure the implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management and ensure full utilization of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO and the MDRRMC complied with the requirements per COA. The MDRRMO, the Accountant, Budget Office and MDRRMC incorporated necessary expenditures in the LDRRMFIP. The office sorted the unspent portion by year and reverted to the General Fund all those that have already lapsed after the 5-year period. The MBO was able to maintain the separate registries as required for the LDRRM funds. Not all mandatory positions were filled; however, some positions were filled.
observations.reason: Not all of the required 4 mandatory positions were filled out.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submits the necessary report to the Accounting Office to establish the amount of RPT and SET Receivable accounts. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconciled their records and made the necessary adjustments. The HRMO posted the Municipal Assessor vacancy and continues efforts to fill-up but no qualified applicant yet.
observations.reason: No qualified applicant for the Municipal Assessor position despite efforts of HRMO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to exert maximum effort in tracing the unidentified balances and effect necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct account balance in the financial statements and provide adequate disclosure pertaining to the prior period adjustment in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to trace the unidentified balances and effect necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct account presentation. Adequate disclosure was provided in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management the following measures:
a) Optimize the implementation of its existing revenue ordinances or update and create new ordinances, if warranted, to greatly increase its resources for a more efficient and effective implementation of its development plans, program objectives and priorities;
b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the amount of delinquent taxes are incorporated in the established receivable amount to be forwarded to the Municipal Accountant as the basis for the setting up of the property RPT and SET Receivable; and
c) Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the procedures provided under the NGAS Manual for LGU in the accounting of RPT/SET.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submits reports and the Municipal Accountant complies with NGAS procedures. Efforts were undertaken to optimize local revenue generation though the absence of a Municipal Assessor affected the full implementation.
observations.reason: The lack of a Municipal Assessor resulted to inefficiencies in property assessments as there is no head assessor to oversee the tax assessment process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management the following measures:
a) Exercise prudence and diligence in the disbursement of funds and enforce strict compliance with regulation on the grant of honoraria in accordance with RA 9184 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 on the grant of honoraria to officers/employees involved in government procurement to prevent excessive and unlawful payments;
b) Limit the payment of honoraria to the source identified in the said Budget Circular; and
c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make sure that savings from the approved local budget were duly approved by the Sanggunian before effecting any payment of honoraria to the members of the BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat chargeable to the General Fund and to further treat collections from the sale of bid documents as receipts in the Trust Fund (TF) books.
observations.managementAction: The Management observed the regulation on the grant of honoraria in accordance with the DBM issuance. The Municipal Accountant ensured that payments were limited to identified source and that savings from approved local budget were duly approved by the Sanggunian before effecting payments. Collections from sale of bid documents were treated as receipts in the Trust Fund books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant keep abreast with COA Circular No. 2012-001, comply with the requirement on the submission of supporting documents, and stop processing disbursement vouchers with incomplete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Claims for terminal benefits were supported with adequate documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. Direct the Property/Supply Officer to:
   • Accomplish the pre-repair inspection report after the conduct of the pre-repair inspection of properties and furnish a copy of this report to COA within five (5) days from the date of inspection, post-evaluation/inspection. Likewise, when the subject properties were already repaired, conduct immediately post-repair inspection and submit a report thereon;
   • Ensure that the damaged/replaced, unserviceable supplies and property are kept in a safe stockroom located within the premises of the Municipality awaiting disposal thereof and disposed of in the manner prescribed by existing rules and regulations; and
   • Direct the Property/Supply Officer to attach a Certificate of Acceptance and Report of Waste Material duly approved by the LCE after preliminary inspection/evaluation has been made for the year.
b. Direct the Accounting Officer to maintain a ledger card for the expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every vehicle owned by the LGU in order to monitor whether the property is economically repairable or not.
observations.managementAction: The Property/Supply Officer accomplished pre-repair inspection reports and furnished reports. A safe stockroom was allocated for the storage of damaged/replaced and unserviceable supplies. Ledger cards for repair and maintenance were set-up. Certificate of Acceptance and Report of Waste Material were attached as required.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of inspection reports; not all repairs were supported with inspection reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Provide the needed appropriation and fill up the vacant Municipal Assessor position for a more effective administration, and to increase and maximize the generation of resources and revenues of the LGU; and
b. Direct the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to publish the vacancy of the position.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO posted the vacancy of the Municipal Assessor position. However, there was no qualified applicant for the position.
observations.reason: No qualified applicant for the position.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the General Services Officer to 

Identify the classification of these lands and see whether these are owned and registered by private individuals or by the Government;
If unregistered, facilitate the acquisition of Torrens Title or any document to show ownership of lands presently occupied by the local government unit; and
If registered, exert extra effort to secure the needed documents required to transfer ownership of those lands or real properties in the name of another entity or individual.
observations.managementAction: No management action.
observations.reason: Lack of documents and manpower.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the General Services Officer to:
Identify the classification of these lands and see whether these are owned and registered by private individuals or by the Government;
If unregistered, facilitate the processing of Torrens Title or any document to show ownership of lands presently occupied by the local government unit; and
If registered, exert extra effort to secure the needed documents required to transfer ownership of those lands or real properties in the name of another entity or individual.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory count over the LGU’s Local Road Network. A physical count of Local Road Network should be done at least once a year and prepare Report on the Physical Count of Road Network System and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor;
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Direct the General Services Officer to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately cause the closure of the dormant accounts and cause the transfer of the balance amounting to P 395,933.50 to the Municipality’s active account under General Fund.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a schedule of all unreleased checks every end of the year to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant, and to direct the latter to make necessary adjustments for the reversion to the respective Cash in Bank accounts the total amount of unreleased checks to reflect the correct balances of these accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Instruct the BAC Secretariat to regularly furnish copies of contract of infrastructure projects to enable the local auditor to conduct the necessary legal and auditorial reviews as well as future reference of COA-TAS in the conduct of technical review and inspection in pursuant to the guidelines on Manual on Contracts Review; and
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee, BAC-Secretariat and BAC –TWG to ensure the timely preparation and submission of contracts and Purchase Orders and make sure that complete supporting documents were properly complied with every project and make the necessary request for inspection of COA engineer upon completion of projects prior to its final payment;
Submit to the audit team the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same. Otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of Suspension.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC to submit the lacking documents as required by COA Circular 2012-001 and Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
Instruct the Municipal Engineer and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure strict compliance with:
Sections 37.2.1 and Sections 39.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 requiring contractor to post performance security upon contract signing to guarantee his faithful performance of his obligations under the contract; and
DPWH Department Order No. 216 on the submission of copy of Diary or Log Book and photographic records with imprinted dates.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Hire additional staff in the Accounting Office to address the delay of the submission of the monthly required Financial Reports; and
Direct both the Municipal Accountant & Municipal Treasurer to submit above mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Ensure thru the Property/Supply Officer/ to accomplish the pre-repair inspection report after the conduct of pre-repair inspection of properties subjected for repairs and furnish a copy of this report to COA Auditor within five (5) days from the date of evaluation/inspection. Likewise, when the subject properties were already repaired, conduct immediately post-repair inspection and submit a report thereon;
Direct the Property/Supply Officer to attach Certificate of Acceptance and Report of Waste Material duly approved by the LCE after preliminary inspection/evaluation has been made;
Direct the Accounting Officer to maintain a ledger card for the expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance of every vehicle owned by the LGU in order to monitor whether the property is economically repairable or not; and
Direct the GSO to ensure that the damaged/replaced, unserviceable supplies and property were:
Kept in a safe stockroom located within the premises of the Municipality awaiting for disposal thereof; and
Disposed of in the manner prescribed by existing rules and regulations

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include appropriations for MOOE for the Office of the MDRRMO in the annual budget to provide sufficient funds for the DRRM programs, projects, and activities; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and DRRM Officer to refrain from using the DRRMF for programs/projects/activities (PPAs) which are not directly related with pre-disaster preparedness, mitigation, and response and rescue operations to ensure that is utilized solely for approved DRRM-related PPAs.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended/unobligated QRF and MF to the Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liability- DRRM account. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the entire LDRRM Fund balances currently reflected in the General Fund, to the Trust Fund books of the LGU.  This can be done by following these entries:
In the General Fund book, the entry is:
While in the Trust Fund book, the entry would be:
Require the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRM Officer to ensure constant reconciliation of their records to establish the validity of the funds reflected in the Financial Statements.

Recommendation:
The following are the recommended actions to the Municipal Mayor:
Fill up the vacant Municipal Assessor position and accordingly provide the needed appropriation for a more effective administration, and to raise and  maximize the generation of resources and revenues of the LGU; and
Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to publish the vacancy of position.

Recommendation:
We recommended the management to:
Direct the members of the GFPS, thru the MSWDO, to spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. They shall conduct examination of the agency’s level of gender mainstreaming or extent of the gender responsiveness of the municipal programs and activities, the level of gender awareness and competence of its personnel and the presence or absence of enabling mechanisms that support gender mainstreaming.
Based on the results of gender audit, the GFPS determine programs, projects and activities that are responsive to gender gaps or issues faced by their women and men employees, as well as their clients and constituents.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

Create an Inventory Committee who will facilitate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of all PPE initially and subsequently every year thereafter.
Instruct the head of the Inventory Committee to prepare promptly and completely the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) thereon.
Instruct the General Services Officer and the Acting Municipal Accountant to maintain prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards respectively and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
observations.managementAction: The management were able to maintain the prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for some of the properties added in the books for the year 2021 onwards.
observations.reason: No records of PPE from year 2016 downwards could not be obtained as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:

Review the composition of the Accounts Payable account to determine if these are valid claims, ensure that these are duly supported with the required documents and reflect only the true and valid payables of the agency,
Revert those accounts found to be without valid claimants and are aged two or more years pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445, and
Recognize Accounts Payable only at the time goods and services are accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received duly supported with.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to submit all contracts and Purchase Orders after perfection within five (5) days to the Audit Team for prompt validation and monitoring of the government programs, projects and activities, and
Adhere strictly with the provisions of laws, rules and regulations as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 in the submission of government contracts and Purchase Orders.
observations.managementAction: Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat was able to submit contracts and Purchase Orders for various projects.
observations.reason: Not all contracts and purchase orders were submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Hire additional manpower in the Municipal Accounting Office in order cope up with the load of work the Office has, and
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and other concerned personnel to implement strict observance of the reglementary period in the submission of financial reports and accounts to the Auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.
observations.reason: Management has submitted their reports but still not in its prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned officials to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the period prescribed, which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.reason: Monthly Reports on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF) of the Municipality were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LDRRM Council to prepare annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan in its prescribed format and include the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of previous years in order to utilize the same to fund LDRRM projects/programs/activities.
observations.reason: The LDRRM Council was able to prepare annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan including the unexpended balance of the said fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the MDRRMO and the Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare the reports and post the same to the appropriate website, bulletin board and/or conspicuous place and to furnish the same to COA.
observations.reason: The management was able to comply.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) the following:

Set-up and/ or update sex-disaggregated data or GAD database,

Integrate GAD PPAs in the regular activities of the LGU and administer the HGDG test on the PPAs, the result of which shall be the basis in the estimating the cost of gender mainstreaming and in the preparation of GPB, and

Prepare GPB and Accomplishment Report following the prescribed format in accordance with JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.reason: The GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report were prepared with the prescribed format in accordance with JMC No. 2016-01.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to the following:
Direct the General Services Officer to facilitate the acquisition of Torrens Title or any document to show ownership for lots presently occupied by the local government unit; and
b. Also, Require the General Services Officer to exert extra effort to secure the needed documents required to transfer ownership of those lots or real properties in the name of another entity or individual.
observations.managementAction: Management has not yet taken a step in the recognition of possible Lands owned. This finding is reiterated this year.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to the following:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the composition of the Accounts Payable account to determine if these are valid claims, ensure that these are duly supported with the required documents and reflect only the true and valid payables of the agency,

Direct the Municipal Accountant to revert those accounts found to be without valid claimants and are aged two or more years pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445, and

Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize Accounts Payable only at the time goods and services are accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received duly supported with sufficient documents.
observations.managementAction: Management has not yet complied with the recommendation, thus is reiterated this year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to use the proper account codes as prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units (RCALGU) in recording and reporting the financial transactions the agency to have a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management still recognizes expenses chargeable to specific expense accounts in Other Operating Expense Account

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Adhere strictly with the provisions of laws, rules and regulations as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 in the submission of government contracts and Purchase Orders, and

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to submit all contracts and Purchase Orders after perfection within five (5) days to the Audit Team for prompt validation and monitoring of the government programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Contracts and Purchase Orders were still not submitted to our office, thus audit finding is reiterated this year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Create an Inventory Committee who will facilitate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of all PPE initially and subsequently every year thereafter.
Instruct the head of the Inventory Committee to prepare promptly and completely the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) thereon.
Instruct the General Services Officer and the Acting Municipal Accountant to maintain prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards respectively and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the General Services Officer to
Identify the classification of these lands and see whether these are owned and registered by private individuals or by the Government;
If unregistered, facilitate the acquisition of Torrens Title or any document to show ownership of lands presently occupied by the local government unit; and
If registered, exert extra effort to secure the needed documents required to transfer ownership of those lands or real properties in the name of another entity or individual.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:
Review the composition of the Accounts Payable account to determine if these are valid claims, ensure that these are duly supported with the required documents and reflect only the true and valid payables of the agency,
Revert those accounts found to be without valid claimants and are aged two or more years pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445, and
Recognize Accounts Payable only at the time goods and services are accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received duly supported with.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to submit all contracts and Purchase Orders after perfection within five (5) days to the Audit Team for prompt validation and monitoring of the government programs, projects and activities, and
Adhere strictly with the provisions of laws, rules and regulations as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 in the submission of government contracts and Purchase Orders.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Hire additional manpower in the Municipal Accounting Office in order cope up with the load of work the Office has, and
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and other concerned personnel to implement strict observance of the reglementary period in the submission of financial reports and accounts to the Auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned officials to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the period prescribed, which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended the LDRRM Council to prepare annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan in its prescribed format and include the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of previous years in order to utilize the same to fund LDRRM projects/programs/activities.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the MDRRMO and the Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare the reports and post the same to the appropriate website, bulletin board and/or conspicuous place and to furnish the same to COA.

We recommended the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) the following:
Set-up and/ or update sex-disaggregated data or GAD database,
Integrate GAD PPAs in the regular activities of the LGU and administer the HGDG test on the PPAs, the result of which shall be the basis in the estimating the cost of gender mainstreaming and in the preparation of GPB, and
Prepare GPB and Accomplishment Report following the prescribed format in accordance with JMC No. 2016-01

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the Supply Officer and RHU to coordinate and come up with a clear defined responsibility for proper inventory-taking, reporting, supervision and disposition of the transferred drugs, medicines, medical supplies and equipment from DOH and PHO.
Require the Supply Officer to inform the Municipal Accountant and furnish her with the receipt of asset for her to recognize it in the books basing on the PTRs issued by the DOH.
The receiving officer should make sure that PTRs are duly accomplished and value of items received are indicated properly otherwise coordinate with the DOH and PHO for that matter.
Distribution and utilization report should be prepared for evaluation and documentation.
Require the Municipal Accountant to record the remaining items on hand in the books of accounts for CY 2020.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following: 
Direct both the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Stock Cards for inventories and the Property Cards for PPEs and subsidiary ledger cards for each property, plant and equipment respectively.
Direct the General Services Officer to:
Conduct physical count of PPEs annually, and
Identify unserviceable properties and prepare an Inspection and Inventory Report for disposal.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to: 
Update the Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC), and
Prepare necessary adjustments on the deficiencies noted in the course of the inventory taking and make reconciliation with inventory report, stock cards and subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: Management has already conducted initial inventory but was not able to finish it due to lack of manpower. The LCE committed to create an Inventory Committee to do the inventory and comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that Transfer, Grants and Donations from other levels of government, private sector or international institutions are properly recognized as revenue, and
Require the Municipal Accountant to inform DOH Regional Office No. X of the proper accounting entries in case of future similar transaction

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the General Services Officer to instruct all drivers to prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels and collect such reports, and
b. Based thereon prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption showing the type of vehicles, total distance traveled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumptions for each vehicle and submit same to the Auditor as required in the Manual to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Adhere strictly with the mandate of RA 656 and provide adequate insurance protection of its property, assets and interest to the General Insurance Fund of GSIS,
Direct the General Services Officer to determine the insurable properties and equipment and submit it for budgeting purposes and requirements, and
c. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the Annual budget the appropriation of amount necessary to cover expenses for insurance premiums.
observations.managementAction: Management has insured their Motor Vehicles but not the other insurable assets.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:

Reconsider staffing in the Municipal Accountant’s Office for additional manpower to enable them to cope up with the numerous tasks entrusted to them,

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the office of the Audit Team the reports not yet submitted as of to date, and

c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to religiously comply with the prescribed period of the submission of the required reports and its supporting documents as prescribed in the COA rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted some of their reports but still not in its prescribed period. Audit Finding is reiterated this year due to its materiality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor to immediately create the agency’s Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in accordance with the provision of law who shall then develop the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan that shall ensure the long-term management of solid waste for the protection of public health and the environment as envisioned in R.A. 9003. We also recommend that the Management continue allocating funds for the implementation of the projects/programs/activities of above-mentioned plan.
observations.managementAction: Management is yet to create Solid waste Management Board who are responsible for management of solid waste.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to the following:
Require the General Supply Officer and RHU to coordinate and come up with a clear defined responsibility for proper inventory-taking, reporting, supervision and disposition of the transferred drugs, medicines, medical supplies and equipment from DOH and PHO.
Require the Supply Officer to inform the Municipal Accountant and furnish her with the receipt of asset for her to recognize it in the books basing on the PTRs issued by the DOH.
The receiving officer should make sure that PTRs are duly accomplished and value of items received are indicated properly otherwise coordinate with the DOH and PHO for that matter.
Distribution and utilization report should be prepared for evaluation and documentation.
Require the Municipal Accountant to record the remaining items on hand in the books of accounts for CY 2020.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct both the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Stock Cards for inventories and the Property Cards for PPEs and subsidiary ledger cards for each property, plant and equipment, respectively;
Require the General Services Officer to:
Conduct physical count of PPEs annually, and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare necessary adjustments on the deficiencies noted in the course of the inventory taking and make reconciliation with inventory report, stock cards and subsidiary ledgers.

Recommendation

Direct the General Services Officer to determine the insurable properties and equipment and submit it for budgeting purposes and requirements, and

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the office of the Audit Team the reports not yet submitted as of to date, and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to religiously comply with the prescribed period of the submission of the required reports and its supporting documents as prescribed in the COA rules and regulations.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare the bank reconciliation statement on a regular and timely basis. Likewise, we require the Municipal Treasurer to obtain from the bank the snapshots of each account right after the end of each month while waiting for the Bank Statements to facilitate the Municipal Accountant’s preparation of BRS.

a. Create an Inventory Committee who will facilitate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of all PPE initially and subsequently every year thereafter.

We recommend the Management to:
Create an Inventory Committee who will facilitate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of all PPE initially and subsequently every year thereafter.
Prepare promptly and completely the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) thereon.
Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Office/Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards respectively and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

We also recommend that the Management continue allocating funds for the implementation of the projects/programs/activities of above-mentioned plan.

the Municipal Accountant to implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 to prepare and submit the monthly BRS within the prescribed period together with the bank statements, paid checks and original copies of debit/credit memos, and

observations.managementAction: The Management has started the reconciliation of the book and bank balances but has not yet submitted them to the Auditor’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to accomplish the RCD’s Section A, B, C and D (as applicable) and submit together with its supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant for recording. Through the Municipal Accountant, submit the said report and its supporting documents to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the Accounting Unit to prepare the corresponding disbursement voucher of the monthly amortization of loan thru ADA.

We recommended the Management to:
Create an inventory committee or task force to conduct physical count of all the existing PPEs;
Require the Property Officer to prepare the Report of Physical Count of PPEs; and
Renew the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment every three years.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officers has initiated inventory proceedings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to make representation with the Provincial Government of Lanao del Norte as regards to the details of this guaranty deposit to determine its accurate amount so that this can be fairly presented in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: Inquiries with concerned offices were made but there was no follow-ups as to the final status of the account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
reclassify or transfer the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF in the amount of P4,843,158 from the General Fund Books to a Special Trust Fund,
deposit the unexpended balance of P3,664,860  of the DRRMF-MOOE to LBP account no. 0802-1109-60 (Calamity Fund) and transfer the same amount from the General Fund Continuing Appropriation to the Special Trust Fund books, the account Trust Liabilities-DRRMF in the Trust Fund books shall be used to record transfers and
consistently follow sections 5.1.10 to 5.1.13 of COA Circular no. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 accounting and reporting guidelines on the unexpended balance of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Management observe the prevailing policies as prescribed by Sections 70 and 71 Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for LGU and COA Circular No. 2005-001 dated February 28, 2005 as restated with amendments by COA Circular No. 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006. And require the Head of the Budget Unit to submit not later than the twentieth day after the end of the month the Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for both the current and continuing appropriations.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations reports were made but was not submitted monthly to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Vice-Mayor to apply for a fidelity bond and, consequently, to countersign the check drawn for disbursement that pertain to transactions of Office of the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended the Management:
to ensure GAD planning and budgeting shall be integrated in the regular activities of the LGUs and
to advise GAD Focal person to submit the Approved Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) by DILG/ PCW and the Accomplishment Report on GAD Activities.
observations.managementAction: Said reports were accomplished and are readily available but copies of which were not yet submitted to the Auditor’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat and/or General Service Officer Designate to submit the required documents previously mentioned.
observations.managementAction: The management is trying all means to submit the required documents.
observations.reason: Since the said transaction transpired during the last administration, gathering required documents was not easy.

observations.managementAction: The management already submitted a draft of SWM Plan however it was not approved since it did not pass the standards set forth.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require:
The Municipal Accountant to implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 to prepare and submit the monthly BRS within the prescribed period together with the bank statements, paid checks and original copies of debit/credit memos.
The Municipal Accountant to request for additional personnel to augment the workforce in the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

Through the Municipal Accountant, submit the said report and its supporting documents to COA.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding disbursement voucher of the monthly amortization of loan thru ADA to support the monthly disbursement/ deduction from the 20% Development Fund out of the monthly Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA).

Create an inventory committee or task force to conduct physical count of all the existing PPEs;

Renew the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment every three years.

observations.managementAction: An inventory committee or task force to conduct physical count of all the existing PPEs was already created.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. The taskforce created have not started yet due to the some circumstances hindering them to begin such as the bonding of the Property officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the supplemental budget an additional allocation of fidelity bond and consequently the property officer to apply for a bond.
observations.managementAction: Bonding of the Property Officer is still on process since her appointed is yet to be approved by the Civil Service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local  Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the prescribed time to submit these reports to COA and for their Local Chief Executive to monitor its compliance.
observations.managementAction: Financial Reports such as Trial Balances were submitted although delayed. The Reports of Accountability for Accountable Forms were only submitted during the cash examination period.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report since the Municipal Accountant failed to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements and the Municipal Budget Officer was delayed in the submission of Status of Appropriation, Allotment, Obligations and Balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local  Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the regulations relative to the submission of monthly reports and supporting documents to COA within ten (10) days from receipt of said documents pursuant to 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat and/or General Service Officer Designate to submit the perfected contracts and purchase orders to COA within five (5) days from its perfection.
observations.managementAction: The Contracts were submitted to COA however beyond the five days prescribed period.
observations.reason: They are trying their best to comply with the submission on or before the five days prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to submit promptly the monthly report of accountable forms as well as the used and issued accountable forms in sequence to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submits the RAAF only when required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require all the municipality’s employees to open a personal bank account for comprehensive electronic payroll system with any accredited government depository bank to lower the risk of fund misuse and result in greater convenience, transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already signed a Memorandum of Agreement with LBP for comprehensive electronic payroll system.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the GAD Focal person to attend seminar on GAD mainstreaming and to ensure that activities identified as GAD are in conformity with the provisions under Philippine Commission on Women  (PCW), NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 12-01 that is to respect, protect  and fulfill the rights of woman at the socio-cultural, economic and  political spheres and to submit the GAD plan and accomplishment reports,  respectively to this Office.

We recommended that the management to do away with the practice of issuing checks to each employee as payments to service rendered to each employee during payday in favor of a comprehensive electronic payroll system to pay the employees to lower the risk of fund misuse and result in greater convenience, transparency and accountability. And documentary requirement like approved DTR, among others should be submitted before a salary of each employee will be paid.
observations.managementAction: They have coordinated with the Land Bank of the Philippines relative to the opening of the employees accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management should require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to strictly observe the prescribed time to submit these reports to COA and for their Local Chief Executive to monitor its compliance.
observations.managementAction: They are exerting efforts but to no avail.
observations.reason: The Acting Accountant has too many workload assigned to her.  Reiterated in this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management should strictly follow the rules and regulations set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit to this office the following documents:
1. Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP)
2. Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF (January to December 2015)
observations.managementAction: No employee assigned to do this report.
observations.reason: Limited manpower.

Create an inventory committee or task force to conduct physical count of all the existing PPEs;
Require the Property Officer to prepare the Report of Physical Count of PPEs; and
Renew the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment every three years.

We recommended that the Local  Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the prescribed time to submit these reports to COA and for their Local Chief Executive to monitor its compliance.

We recommended the Local  Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the regulations relative to the submission of monthly reports and supporting documents to COA within ten (10) days from receipt of said documents pursuant to 6.05 of COA	Circular No. 95-006.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat and/or General Service Officer Designate to submit the perfected contracts and purchase orders to COA within five (5) days from its perfection.
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat and/or General Service Officer Designate to submit the required documents previously mentioned.
We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to submit promptly the monthly report of accountable forms as well as the used and issued accountable forms in sequence to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require all the municipality’s employees to open a personal bank account for comprehensive electronic payroll system with any accredited government depository bank to lower the risk of fund misuse and result in greater convenience, transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the management make a representation to the higher authority that the funds for the future proposed BUB projects will released sooner and be prioritized as it will affect the performance of the LGU and the constituent’s livelihood upliftment will be delayed and compromised.

We have recommended to the GAD Focal person to attend seminar on GAD mainstreaming and to ensure that activities identified as GAD are in conformity with the provisions under Philippine Commission on Women 	(PCW), NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 12-01 that is to respect, protect 	and fulfill the rights of woman at the socio-cultural, economic and 	political spheres and to submit the GAD plan and accomplishment reports, 	respectively to this Office.

We recommended that the municipal treasurer and concerned Municipality Officials adhere and comply with the Department Order No. 27-05 of the DOF and that the PNB accounts deposits be closed and transferred to any AGDB either in the LBP or DBP at the municipality’s discretion, putting however into consideration the security, safety and convenience of all concerned.
observations.managementAction: They have started with the process of closing their remaining deposit with the Philippine National Bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management to attach the document required as evidence of receipt of payment or remittance hereof.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the official receipts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to record the purchase of supplies and materials for stocks regardless whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period to inventory account wherein a control account shall be maintained in the General Ledger on a current basis.
Require the Chief Accountant to maintain the perpetual inventory records comprising of Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) for each commodity/stock, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) for each category of plant, property and equipment and Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card (WOABSLC) for each type of livestock. Such ledger cards shall contain the details of the property, plant and equipment and livestock account in the inventory control account in the general ledger.
Require the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, to consolidate weekly the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI).  The SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS shall be submitted to the Chief Accountant, who shall compute cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method. Based on the SSMI, a JEV shall be prepared to record the expenditures using appropriate expenditure accounts.
observations.managementAction: They have refrain from charging it to an expense account their drugs and medicines procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer should comply strictly with the preparation and submission of required reports enumerated in Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management tried to minimize their spending on payment for repairs and maintenance specifically on motor vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the above enumerated documents be submitted for auditorial and technical review, respectively and to facilitate further the technical inspection/ evaluation of the COA Regional Technical Services of the said project.
observations.managementAction: Trying to gather the other documents relating to this project.
observations.reason: Voluminous work.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency through their Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) must adhere to the guidelines in the procurement of goods, supplies and materials, pursuant to provisions of RA 9184 and Generic Procurement Manual, Volume II. Procurement of goods, infrastructure projects and consulting services must be subjected to public bidding to ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained and that the contract will be awarded to the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Responsive Bidder/ Highest Rate Responsive Bid or Single Calculated/ Rated Responsive Bid, as the case maybe.
observations.managementAction: They committed to conduct public bidding on their future projects.
observations.reason: Due to peace and order situation of the municipality wherein limited  bidders signify their interest to participate.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management must be compliant to the provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184. 
Likewise, please submit the following documents to enable us to make a decision in audit:
1. Approved APP or any amendments thereto.
2. PPMPs in support to the APP
observations.managementAction: Management was trying to prepare APP.
observations.reason: Lacked adequate planning.
